ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / KANSAS

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Kansas Dental Assistant Registration

Kansas Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in Kansas run through Kansas Dental Board. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Not required for most applicants.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Kansas does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

  1. STEP 1

    Create Account and Submit Online Application

    Visit the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal (kansas.professions.kcc.ks.gov), create a new user account, and then complete and submit the Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) application form online.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Submit Required Documentation

    Upload proof of graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program OR documentation of at least 1,000 hours of clinical practice as a dental assistant and completion of a Board-approved EDDA course. Additionally, provide proof of current Basic Life Support (BLS) or CPR certification, and verification of training for each specific expanded duty (e.g., coronal polishing, sealants, nitrous oxide monitoring) you intend to perform.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Pay Application Fee

    Remit the non-refundable $75 application fee directly through the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal upon submission of your application.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Pass Jurisprudence Examination

    Access and successfully complete the Kansas Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically available through the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal once your application is submitted.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Board Review and Approval

    The Kansas Dental Board will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. Upon successful verification and approval, your EDDA registration will be issued.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$0
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$0

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Kansas Dental Board
Phone
(785) 296-6400
Website
dental.ks.gov/
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in Kansas follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (expires October 1st of even-numbered years)
Renewal fee
$75
Continuing education
12 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Kansas require a dental assistant license?

No, Kansas does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

Is DANB certification recognized in Kansas?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Kansas.

Can I take dental x-rays in Kansas?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Kansas?

There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Kansas. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

At a glance

Processing time

Fingerprinting

Not required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

See board

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years (expires October 1st of even-numbered years) · $75

Licensing board

Kansas Dental Board
